channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Drug Safety & Pharmacovigilance
channel.description: IBD literature monitoring: Drug Safety & Pharmacovigilance

item.title: Adverse Events and Safety Outcomes Associated With Chemotherapy for Inflammatory Bowel Disease-associated Gastrointestinal Cancers.
item.description: Anticancer research - Management of inflammatory bowel disease-associated gastrointestinal cancers, including Crohn's disease-associated cancer and ulcerative colitis-associated cancer, remains challenging because of co...

item.title: STING pathway contributes to Steroid-Hyporesponsive Lung Inflammation in DSS-induced colitis mice model.
item.description: PloS one - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), a chronic inflammation of the gastrointestinal tract, is well recognized for triggering extraintestinal manifestations, including pulmonary complications. Emerging...

item.title: Managing Acute Severe Ulcerative Colitis in the Older Patient: A Growing Concern.
item.description: Drugs & aging - Review - Since the prevalence of older patients with ulcerative colitis is increasing, hospitalizations for severe disease in this age group are expected to rise. Older patients are more likely to present w...

item.title: Longitudinal Safety Outcomes of Anti-tumor Necrosis Factor Alpha Therapy in the Treatment of Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Short- and medium-term safety profiles of anti-TNF therapies are well-established, but data on their long-term safety, especially beyond five years, remain limited. This study aims to compare the r...

item.title: Positive conversion of latent tuberculosis screening in patients with inflammatory bowel disease on antitumor necrosis factor alpha drugs: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) patients undergoing antitumor necrosis factor-alpha (anti-TNF) therapy are at increased risk of developing tuberculosis (TB), making screening before anti-TNF initi...

item.title: Functional Evaluation of Computationally Designed IL-10 in IL-10 KO Mice.
item.description: Biomolecules - Studies have shown that IL-10 has therapeutic potential for inflammatory diseases. However, it is challenging to use IL-10 as a therapeutic drug because it also possesses pro-inflammatory functions...

item.title: Clinical Case Report on the Use of Rezafungin in Pneumocystis jirovecii Pneumonia in a Critically Ill Patient.
item.description: Microorganisms - Case report - Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia (PJP) is a life-threatening opportunistic infection predominantly affecting immunocompromised patients. Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (TMP-SMX) remains the standard...

item.title: Preventive Effect of Chenopodium formosanum Koidz. on Dextran Sulfate Sodium-Induced Chronic Colitis in Mice.
item.description: Nutrients - Background: Chenopodium formosanum Koidz. (djulis) is an indigenous cereal crop native to Taiwan, and its effects on patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) warrant exploration. The present ...

item.title: Chest X-ray prior to biological treatment in inflammatory bowel diseases: it is not only about tuberculosis - a case report and literature review.
item.description: European journal of gastroenterology & hepatology - Biological treatments have substantially improved management of inflammatory bowel disease, but their profound immunosuppressive effects necessitate infection screening before initiation. Current g...

item.title: Identification of Drug Repurposing Opportunities of Immunomodulatory Drugs for Inflammatory Bowel Disease Through Inverse Pharmacovigilance Signal Detection in the FAERS Database.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background/Objectives: Drug repurposing represents a promising strategy to expand therapeutic options for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), a chronic condition with persistent unmet clinical needs....
